coredata.dc:title: RETRACTED: Ions-induced two-photon fluorescence dual-switching for reversible and simultaneous sensing of Cu2+ and Hg2+ based on dual-emitting carbon dot/carbon dot conjugates
coredata.dc:creator: Gui, Rijun
Jin, Hui
Wang, Yanfeng
Sun, Jie
coredata.dc:description: This article has been retracted: please see Elsevier Policy on Article Withdrawal (https://www.elsevier.com/about/our-business/policies/article-withdrawal). This article has been retracted at the request of the Editor following concerns raised by a reader. The article uses several electron micrographs which have been used in other publications as well, denoting different samples. Fig. 1B is identical to Fig. 2A in Talanta vol 176, pp. 277-283 and Fig. 1A in Materials Letters vol. 195, pp. 131-135 while describing different samples. Fig. 1C appears to contain repeated particle clusters. Fig. 1D is identical to Fig. 2B in The Analyst, 2014, 139, 3398-3406 while describing different samples. Fig. 2 The overhang in the FT-IR spectra of carboxyl-modified CD2 at 1500cm−1 indicates that that the data has been manipulated. These problems with the data presented cast doubt on all the data, and accordingly also the conclusions based on that data, in this publication. As such this article represents a severe abuse of the scientific publishing system. The scientific community takes a very strong view on this matter and apologies are offered to readers of the journal that this was not detected during the submission process.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Sensors and Actuators B 245 (2017) 386â€“394 Contents lists available at ScienceDirect Sensors and Actuators B: Chemical jo u r nal homep age: www.elsev ier .com/ locate /snb Ions-in wit and sim sed carbon Rijun Gu a College of Che e Gro University, Qin b Institute of M a r t i c l Article history: Received 29 N Received in re Accepted 25 Ja Available onlin Keywords: Carbon dots Two-photon ï¬‚uorescence Simultaneous sensing Dual-emitting Switching ot (C 1 wit phot ies a (0â€“3.2 M) due to inner ï¬�lter effect, with a linear coefï¬�cient (R2) of 0.9933 and a limit of detection (LOD) of 0.05 M. The TPF peak intensities at 600 nm were almost linearly reduced by Hg2+ (0â€“8.5 M, R2 = 0.9970, LOD = 0.08 M) originating from the nonradiative recombination of excitons through elec- tron transfer. Notably, the TPF peak intensities at 465 and 600 nm could be near wholly recovered by respective addition of S2âˆ’ and Iâˆ’. Thus, the CD1/CD2 conjugates were developed as a novel TPF dual- switching sensor for reversible and simultaneous sensing of Cu2+ and Hg2+. This novel sensor enabled highly selective and sensitive detection of Cu2+ and Hg2+ over other metal ions and biological molecules 1. Introdu In 2004, the separat [1]. Since t ing various wide appli nanoparticl cores with or graphen hybridised quantum d tages such a inertness. B preparation emission, fa photochem âˆ— Correspon E-mail add http://dx.doi.o 0925-4005/Â© in real water samples and biological ï¬‚uids, with high detection recoveries. Â© 2017 Elsevier B.V. All rights reserved. ction Xu et al. ï¬�rstly discovered carbon dots (CDs) during ion and puriï¬�cation of single-walled carbon nanotubes hen, numerous efforts have been devoted to exploit- synthetic methods, physicochemical properties and cations of CDs [2]. CDs are typically quasi-spherical es (NPs) that consist of amorphous to nanocrystalline predominantly graphitic or turbostratic (sp2) carbon e/graphene oxide sheets fused by diamond-like sp3 carbon insertions [3â€“5]. Compared with semiconductor ots containing heavy metals, CDs have marked advan- s low toxicity, biocompatibility, lost cost and chemical esides, the unique characters of CDs include simple , benign chemical composition, tunable ï¬‚uorescence cile functionalization, excellent physicochemical and ical stability, etc [6]. Abundant carboxyl and hydroxyl ding author. ress: guirijun@163.com (R. Gui). moieties around the surface of CDs impart high water solubility and suitable chemically reactive groups for further functionalization or surface passivation [3,7]. As a class of newly emerging nanomateri- als, CDs have a broad range of promising applications, especially in chem./bio-sensing, bio-imaging, drug delivery, photodynamic therapy, photocatalysis and electrocatalysis [2,8â€“14]. Except for conventional down-conversion ï¬‚uorescence, previ- ous researches on up-conversion ï¬‚uorescence of CDs have been reported in recent years [15â€“19]. The up-conversion ï¬‚uorescence emission is an interesting optical phenomenon wherein the emis- sion wavelength is shorter than its excitation wavelength, which is particularly suitable for sensing and imaging in biological samples [20]. When excited by a femtosecond (fs) pulsed laser with a wave- length in near-infrared (NIR) region (with low energy and deep penetration), the up-conversion ï¬‚uorescence is also termed as two- photon ï¬‚uorescence (TPF) [21]. The use of TPF in biological samples can avoid potential autoï¬‚uorescence and background ï¬‚uorescence from biological species, which allows for sensitive bio-sensing and high resolution bio-imaging [22]. In 2007, Cao et al. ï¬�rst found TPF phenomenon of CDs that emitted visible light when excited with an 800 nm fs pulsed laser [15]. Afterward, numerous researches rg/10.1016/j.snb.2017.01.166 2017 Elsevier B.V. All rights reserved. RE TR Aduced two-photon ï¬‚uorescence dual-s ultaneous sensing of Cu2+ and Hg2+ ba dot/carbon dot conjugates ia,âˆ—, Hui Jina, Yanfeng Wangb, Jie Sunb mistry and Chemical Engineering, Laboratory of Fiber Materials and Modern Textile, Th gdao 266071, PR China ateria Medica, Shandong Academy of Medical Sciences, Jinan 250062, PR China e i n f o ovember 2016 vised form 19 January 2017 nuary 2017 e 29 January 2017 a b s t r a c t Dual-emitting carbon dot/carbon d poly(ethylenimine)-functionalized CD the CD1/CD2 conjugates showed two- 600 nm (CD2). The TPF peak intensitching for reversible on dual-emitting wing Base for State Key Laboratory, Qingdao D1/CD2) conjugates were prepared from combination of h carboxyl-modiï¬�ed CD2. When excited with a laser of 800 nm, on ï¬‚uorescence (TPF) emissions peaked at 465 nm (CD1) and t 465 nm decreased near linearly after the addition of Cu2+TE D R. Gui et al. / Sensors and Actuators B 245 (2017) 386â€“394 387 have referred to TPF of CDs due to its unique optical properties and broad applications [23â€“26]. During the past decade, previous lit- eratures about the TPF or up-conversion ï¬‚uorescence of CDs have mainly focused on cell imaging [23â€“31]. So far, only two literatures have referre et al. develo hypochlorit of CDs exci probe by us live cells an In this w reversible a dual-emitti vious resea colorimetri ions. Hence induced TP sensing of C 2. Experim 2.1. Aqueous suspension of CD1 was treated by evaporating water and precipitated with ace- tone. The precipitates were collected by centrifugation and washed with ethanol. After three times repeated treatments, puriï¬�ed pre- es w as di . boxy f cit 53]. Yin ped a CDs-based TPF probe of hypochlorite, based on the e-induced TPF (up-conversion ï¬‚uorescence) quenching ted at 780 nm [32]. Zhu et al. reported a TPF turn-on ing CDs as a platform for hydrogen sulï¬�de sensing in d tissues, excited at 800 nm [33]. ork, we developed a new type of TPF sensor that enabled nd simultaneous sensing of Cu2+ and Hg2+, based upon ng carbon dot/carbon dot (CD1/CD2) conjugates. Pre- rches have mentioned simultaneous (e.g. ï¬‚uorescent, c, electrochemical) sensing of dual or multiple targeted f them depend on a single-molecule sensor that is able re than one species in different signal channels, with- rference between analytes [34â€“43]. Besides, ï¬‚uorescent A-Ag nanoclusters, aptamer-modiï¬�ed AuNPs or upcon- , dual-excitation graphene quantum dots) were used for us sensing of dual ions [44â€“47]. To date, there is still no mentions to TPF simultaneous sensing of dual or mul- g2Â± is one of the most toxic heavy metal ions and can le toxicity or damage to human body. Cu2Â± at a high on can lead to serious damage to the liver and kidney. he simultaneous detection of two or more toxic ions Â±) with only one sensor is simple, efï¬�cient and imper- ially when dual or multiple ions potentially coexist in amples or biological systems. the unique optical properties of TPF and CDs, the fab- D1/CD2 conjugates- based TPF sensor for simultaneous ual ions is a signiï¬�cant work. As illustrated in Scheme 1, hylenimine)-functionalized CDs (PEI-CD1) were com- carboxyl- modiï¬�ed CDs (CD2) to form dual-emitting njugates through carboxyl-amino reaction. Cu2+ can by âˆ’NH2 groups from PEI-CD1 to yield an absorbent the surface of CD1 [48], inducing TPF decrease of CD1 lter effect. Then, S2âˆ’ can combine Cu2+ to form stable ], which induced the separation of Cu2+ from CD1 sur- llowing TPF recovery of CD1. Carboxyl groups on CD2 lated with Hg2+, which accelerated the nonradiative ion of excitons through electron transfer [50] and led ial TPF decrease of CD2. In Fig. 1a, the TEM image of CD1 revealed sed NPs with a spherical morphology and a size dis- f 3.5â€“9.5 nm. Carboxyl-modiï¬�ed CD2 was prepared by al treatment of citric acid and urea [53]. TEM image TE D CD2 showed almost spherical NPs with a size in the 3.0â€“10.5 nm. After the conjugation of CD1 with CD2 bodiimide-activated coupling [54], CD1/CD2 conjugates red and presented peanut-like and near monodisperse s in TEM image (Fig. 1c). LOD and linear ranges from TPF sensing of Cu2+ and work were comparable or superior to previous ï¬‚uores- ods [48â€“51,58], The integration of TPF and simultaneous dual-ions would be much suitable for actual detection n complicated samples or biological systems. c indicated that CD1/CD2 conjugates could serve as a sor for simultaneous TPF sensing of Cu2+ and Hg2+. Sig- he conjugates-based TPF sensor showed a capacity of ensing of dual-ions. In Fig. 5d, the relative TPF intensity was reduced by 0.5 M Cu2+ (b), and it then increased nitial intensity after the addition of 0.5 M S2âˆ’ (c). Sim- TPF recoveries were all more than 97.5% (from d to i) dition of Cu2+ and S2âˆ’, revealing the high reversibility TPF intensity at 465 nm. In addition, Fig. 5e illustrated rsibility of relative TPF peak intensity at 600 nm. More of TPF recoveries were obtained from the addition of (from bâ€™ to iâ€™). Based on ï¬�xed concentrations of differ- the lower-middle of Fig. 5) and TPF recoveries, [S2âˆ’] u2+]) and [Iâˆ’] (equal to 4[Hg2+]) would be highly feasi- rsible and simultaneous TPF sensing of Cu2+ (0â€“3.2 M) â€“8.5 M). ity and sensitivity of TPF dual-switching sensors stigate the selectivity and sensitivity of CD1/CD2 based TPF dual-switching sensor of Cu2+ and Hg2+, nï¬‚uences from various metal ions and biological on TPF responses of the sensor were evaluated. As a relative TPF intensities (at 465 nm) of CD1/CD2 conju- yed a highly selective decrease over various metal ions u2+ under the same conditions (Fig. 6). Notably, other tal ions only produced slight effects (< 0.14) on the rel- tensities at 465 nm. By contrast, there is a dramatic 0.92) from Cu2+ to TPF peak intensities, attributed to ctive inner ï¬�lter effect from Cu2+-amine complex that rb the TPF emission of CD1 to trigger TPF quenching ition, highly selective TPF decrease toward Hg2+ in rel- tensities (at 600 nm) was observed in Fig. 6. Potential from other kinds of metal ions are negligible (<0.09). g2+ caused a marked TPF decrease (0.95) under the same High selectivity of CD2 to Hg2+ is ascribed to interactions g2+ and carboxyl of CD2, which accelerated the non- combination of excitions via electron transfer process thus leading to a substantial TPF decrease of CD2. l inï¬‚uences from biological molecules on TPF responses or were evaluated. Biological molecules (such as his- teine, glutathione, glucose, uric acid, ascorbic acid, ylic acid) were investigated because of their potential in biological ï¬‚uids. As a result, potential inï¬‚uences ind of biological molecules on relative TPF intensities of njugates (peaked at 465 and 600 nm) were slight (less nd 0.02) (Fig. 7). Under the same conditions, dramatic 0.91 for Cu2+, 0.92 for Hg2+) in the relative TPF inten- 5 and 600 nm were observed. Upon the coexistence of al molecules, Cu2+ and Hg2+ still enabled dramatic TPF or 0.95 and 0.96, respectively. These results conï¬�rmed ivity and sensitivity of the TPF dual-switching sensor + and Hg2+, over other metal ions or molecules in bio- s. TE D R. Through carboxyl- bination of PEI-functionalized CD1 with carboxyl- 2, CD1/CD2 conjugates were prepared. When excited by fs laser, the conjugates had two TPF emissions peaked from CD1) and 600 nm (from CD2). After the addition form Cu2+-amine complex, TPF intensities at 465 nm bviously because the complex could absorb TPF emis- TE D1 to induce TPF quenching via inner ï¬�lter effect. In PF intensities at 600 nm were remarkably reduced by o the chelating of Hg2+ with carboxyl of CD2, which the nonradiative recombination of excitons via elec- er, and thus resulted in a substantial TPF decrease of e TPF intensities at 465 nm and 600 nm decreased near h increasing [Cu2+] and [Hg2+] in the range of 0â€“3.2 M M, together with low LOD of 0.05 M Cu2+ and 0.08 M ensor enabled reversible sensing of Cu2+ and Hg2+ after of S2âˆ’ ([S2âˆ’]/[Cu2+] = 1) and Iâˆ’ ([Iâˆ’]/[Hg2+] = 4). Exper- ults proved the high selectivity and sensitivity of the ard Cu2+ and Hg2+, over other metal ions and biological Detected values of [Cu2+] and [Hg2+] from the sensor agreement with spiked values in real water samples cal ï¬‚uids, accompanying with high detection recover- 104.50%) and low RSD values (0.89â€“4.85%). The high low LOD and broad detection range of this TPF sensor quirements from environmental, industrial and biolog- ring applications. These results adequately testiï¬�ed the lity of CD1/CD2 conjugates serving as ions-induced TPF ing for reversible and simultaneous sensing of Cu2+ and l samples. gments rk was ï¬�nancially supported by the National Natu- Foundation of China (21305079), the Natural Science R. Biographies Rijun Gui received his BS degree in 2005, and completed his MS degree in applied chemistry from Shanghai Ocean University in 2009. He then studied at East China University of Science and Technology to pursue a Ph.D. degree in physical chemistry. After completing his Ph.D. degree in 2012, he works as a postdoctoral research fellow at Shanghai Jiao Tong University. In 2014, he joined to Qingdao University, where he is currently an assistant professor. His main researches focus on the synthesis, properties and applications of ï¬‚uorescent nanomaterials. Hui Jin was born in 1983. She obtained her Ph.D. from Tongji University, China. Now, she is an assistant professor in College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Qingdao University, China. Her researches focus on the preparation of ï¬‚uorescent nanomaterials for chemo./bio-sensing and imaging applications. Yanfeng Wang obtained her Ph.D. from Shandong University, China. Currently, she works as an assistant professor in Institute of Materia Medica, Shandong Academy of Medical Sciences. Her researches focus on the synthesis and applications of ï¬‚uo- rescent nanomaterials. Jie Sun received his Ph.D. from Guangzhou Institute of Chemistry, Chinese Academic of Science. Currently, he works as an assistant professor in Institute of Materia Med- ica, Shandong Academy of Medical Sciences. His research interests focus on the synthesis, properties and applications of ï¬‚uorescent nanomaterials. ET RA CT EDR pconversion nanoparticles and controlled particles for the simultaneous detection of Pb2+ and Hg2+, Talanta 4) 327â€“336. . Wang, G. Li, C. Chen, Y. Chi, G. Chen, Polyamine-functionalized antum dots as ï¬‚uorescent probes for selective and sensitive of copper ions, Anal. Chem. 84 (2012) 6220â€“6224. . Li, K. Lv, W. Zhong, H. Wang, Z. Wu, et al., Cyclam-functionalized ts sensor for sensitive and selective detection of copper(II) ion and ion in aqueous media and its imaging in live cells, Sens. Actuators B 6) 298â€“306. . Lu, J. Deng, H. Li, Y. Zhang, One-pot electrochemical synthesis of lized ï¬‚uorescent carbon dots and their selective sensing for mercury . Chim. Acta 866 (2015) 69â€“74. hang, J. Zou, Y. Liu, J. Zhuang, Y. Xiao, et al., Carbon dots-based nt probe for off-on sensing of Hg(II) and Iâˆ’ , Biosens. Bioelectron. 79 1â€“535. . Wang, H. Li, J. Shao, Y. Chi, X. Lin, et al., Polyamine-functionalized antum dots for chemical sensing, Carbon 50 (2012) 2810â€“2815. TE D 394 R. Gui et al. / Sensors and Actuators B 245 (2017) 386â€“394 [53] S. Qu, D. Zhou, D. Li, W. Ji, P. Jing, D. Han, et al., Toward efï¬�cient orange emissive carbon nanodots through conjugated sp2-domain controlling and surface charges engineering, Adv. Mater. 28 (2016) 3516â€“3521. [54] C. Wang, H. Lin, Z. Xu, Y. Huang, M.G.

simple-head.ce:title: RETRACTED: Ions-induced two-photon fluorescence dual-switching for reversible and simultaneous sensing of Cu2+ and Hg2+ based on dual-emitting carbon dot/carbon dot conjugates

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Laboratory of Fiber Materials and Modern Textile, The Growing Base for State Key Laboratory, Qingdao University, Qingdao 266071, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Laboratory of Fiber Materials and Modern Textile, The Growing Base for State Key Laboratory, Qingdao University

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Institute of Materia Medica, Shandong Academy of Medical Sciences, Jinan 250062, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Institute of Materia Medica, Shandong Academy of Medical Sciences

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: This article has been retracted: please see Elsevier Policy on Article Withdrawal (https://www.elsevier.com/about/our-business/policies/article-withdrawal).
This article has been retracted at the request of the Editor following concerns raised by a reader.
The article uses several electron micrographs which have been used in other publications as well, denoting different samples.
Fig. 1B is identical to Fig. 2A in Talanta vol 176, pp. 277-283 and Fig. 1A in Materials Letters vol. 195, pp. 131-135 while describing different samples.
Fig. 1C appears to contain repeated particle clusters.
Fig. 1D is identical to Fig. 2B in The Analyst, 2014, 139, 3398-3406 while describing different samples.
Fig. 2 The overhang in the FT-IR spectra of carboxyl-modified CD2 at 1500cm−1 indicates that that the data has been manipulated.
These problems with the data presented cast doubt on all the data, and accordingly also the conclusions based on that data, in this publication.

As such this article represents a severe abuse of the scientific publishing system. The scientific community takes a very strong view on this matter and apologies are offered to readers of the journal that this was not detected during the submission process.
